title: Meistera elephantorum
intro: Meistera elephantorum, also known as Indian Pennywort, is a perennial plant species native to the Western Ghats of India, characterized by its small, rounded leaves and distinctive, penny-shaped flowers. The health benefits of Meistera elephantorum include its potential in reducing inflammation, improving cognitive function, and treating various diseases such as diabetes and cardiovascular disorders. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Meistera elephantorum is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are consumed to harness its therapeutic benefits.
